title: Plasma isolation from whole mouse
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a7qnj","text":"Protocol describing plasma isolation of adult mice. Used in Palumbos et al., 2025. Adapted from Goldsmith et al., 2022.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1nad1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5kkfq","text":"All mouse handling and euthanasia must follow IACUC regulations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f521k","text":"Prior approval from an Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) or equivalent ethics committee is required for this experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Animal is euthanized following IACUC protocol (e.g., isopropanol overdose)
Trunk blood is immediately collected in EDTA-treated tube.
Centrifuge collected blood in EDTA-treated tube at 2,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4C
Following centrifugation, transfer liquid component into clean polypropylene tube
Plasma can be stored at this time at -20C
Add 4X denaturing buffer to diluted sample
a. *Due to the high protein content of plasma, increasing denaturing boil time aids in protein separation
Samples are ready for protein loading
a. For best success, leave empty lane (1X denaturing buffer only) between plasma samples
creator: Sierra Palumbos
